sections.paragraph:
ὅρκος, ου, ὁ (Hom.+) oath Hb 6:16. ὅρκον ὀμνύειν swear an oath (Hyperid. 5, 1; Lucian, Dial. Mer. 2, 1; PHal 1, 226; 230 ὀμόσας τὸν ὅρκον) Js 5:12 (Delphic commands: SIG 1268 I, 8 [III b.c.] ὅρκῳ μὴ χρῶ). ὅρκῳ ὀμνύειν τινί swear to someone with an oath (TestJud 22:3; ApcMos 19) Ac 2:30. ὅρ. ὸ̔ν ὤμοσεν πρὸς Ἀβραάμ Lk 1:73 (cp. OGI 266, 19 ὅρκος ὸ̔ν ὤμοσεν Παράνομος; for the foll. inf. w. the art. cp. Pel.-Leg. p. 13, 9 ἐν ὅρκῳ εἶχεν τοῦ μὴ γεύσασθαί τι). ὅρ. ψευδής a false oath (Theoph. Ant. 2, 34 [p. 186, 10]) 2:8 (Zech 8:17). ἀποδιδόναι τῷ κυρίῳ τοὺς ὅρκους perform oaths to the Lord Mt 5:33 (s. ἀποδίδωμι 2c. But ἀποδοῦναι τινι ὅρκον also means ‘give an oath’: Demosth. 19, 318; Aeschin. 3, 74; SIG 150, 15). μεσιτεύειν ὅρκῳ guarantee by means of an oath Hb 6:17. μεθʼ ὅρκου with an oath (PRev 42, 17 [258 b.c.] μεθʼ ὅρκου; Lev 5:4: Num 30:11; Cornutus 24 p. 46, 8 μεθʼ ὅρκων; Just., D. 33, 2) Mt 14:7; 26:72; 1 Cl 8:2.—Pl. ὅρκη, even when basically only one oath is involved (cp. X., Hell. 5, 4, 54; Diod S 4, 46, 4; 17, 84, 1; Polyaenus 2, 19; Athen. 13, 557a; 2 Macc 4:34; 7:24; 14:32; EpArist 126; Jos., Ant. 3, 272; 7, 294) διὰ τοὺς ὅρκους because of his oath Mt 14:9; Mk 6:26.—ESanders, Jewish Law fr. Jesus to the Mishnah ’90, 51–57, 337f (note). Lit. on ὀμνύω Kl. Pauly II, 209f.—B. 1438. DELG s.v. ὅρκος. M-M. TW. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PHal PHal = Dikaiomata: in a papyrus published by the Graeca Halensis—List 4

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

foll. foll. = followed, following

inf. inf. = infinitive

art. art. = article

Pel.-Leg. Pel.-Leg. = Legenden der heiligen Pelagia, V? a.d.—List 5

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

PRev PRev = Revenue Laws of Ptolemy Philadelphus—List 4

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

Mishnah Mishnah = HDanby, The Mishnah—Lists 5, 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 723
